questions.q: By what poetic title is Sarojini Naidu popularly known?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu is popularly known as the Nightingale of India.

questions.q: Sarojini Naidu was the first Indian woman to become president of which body?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu was the first Indian woman to become president of the Indian National Congress.

questions.q: Besides being a freedom fighter, Sarojini Naidu was also celebrated as a:
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu was a celebrated poet as well as a leader of the freedom movement.

questions.q: In which year did Sarojini Naidu become president of the Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu became president of the Indian National Congress in 1925.

questions.q: Sarojini Naidu led which famous 1930 salt protest?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu led the Dharasana salt raid in 1930.

questions.q: After independence, Sarojini Naidu became the Governor of which region?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu became Governor of the United Provinces after independence.

questions.q: Sarojini Naidu was a skilled public speaker, often praised as a great:
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu was renowned as a powerful orator.

questions.q: Sarojini Naidu was a close associate and follower of which national leader?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu was a close associate and follower of Mahatma Gandhi.

questions.q: The Dharasana raid that Sarojini Naidu led was part of which larger campaign?
options: The salt satyagraha / Civil Disobedience Movement
questions.explanation: The Dharasana raid was part of the salt satyagraha within the Civil Disobedience Movement of 1930.

questions.q: Sarojini Naidu became the first woman to hold which post in independent India?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu became the first woman to serve as Governor of an Indian province (the United Provinces).

questions.q: In which city was Sarojini Naidu born in 1879?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu was born in Hyderabad in 1879.

questions.q: Which famous early collection of Sarojini Naidu's poems was published in 1905?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu's first major poetry collection, The Golden Threshold, was published in 1905.

questions.q: Sarojini Naidu took over the leadership of the Dharasana salt raid after the arrest of which leader?
options: Abbas Tyabji and Gandhi having been detained
questions.explanation: She helped lead the Dharasana raid after Gandhi and Abbas Tyabji were detained during the salt campaign.

questions.q: Over which Congress session did Sarojini Naidu preside as president?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu presided over the 1925 Kanpur session of the Congress.

questions.q: Sarojini Naidu accompanied Gandhi to which event in London in 1931?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu accompanied Gandhi to the Second Round Table Conference in London in 1931.

questions.q: Sarojini Naidu's birthday, 13 February, is commemorated in India as which day?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu's birthday, 13 February, is observed in India as National Women's Day.

questions.q: Before her political career, Sarojini Naidu studied in England at which institutions?
options: King's College London and Girton College, Cambridge
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu studied at King's College London and later at Girton College, Cambridge.

questions.q: Sarojini Naidu was actively involved in which campaign that opposed an act allowing detention without trial in 1919?
options: The agitation against the Rowlatt Act
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu was active in the agitation against the Rowlatt Act in 1919.

questions.q: Sarojini Naidu was a strong advocate for which social cause alongside the freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu was a prominent advocate for women's rights and welfare.

questions.q: Sarojini Naidu joined the Indian freedom movement more actively after which 1905 colonial decision?
questions.explanation: The 1905 Partition of Bengal drew Sarojini Naidu more deeply into the freedom movement.

questions.q: Sarojini Naidu participated in which 1942 mass movement, for which she was imprisoned?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu took part in the 1942 Quit India Movement and was imprisoned along with other leaders.

questions.q: Sarojini Naidu was sometimes called the 'Nightingale' because of what?
questions.explanation: She earned the title Nightingale of India for her lyrical poetry and eloquent oratory.

questions.q: In which year did Sarojini Naidu die while serving as Governor of the United Provinces?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu died in 1949 while serving as Governor of the United Provinces.

questions.q: Sarojini Naidu married Govindarajulu Naidu, a union notable in that era for being:
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu's marriage to Govindarajulu Naidu was an inter-caste marriage, then considered bold for the time.

questions.q: Which 1912 poetry collection by Sarojini Naidu included the well-known poem 'In the Bazaars of Hyderabad'?
questions.explanation: Her 1912 collection The Bird of Time contained the famous poem 'In the Bazaars of Hyderabad'.

questions.q: Sarojini Naidu's daughter, who was also a freedom fighter and later a Governor, was named:
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu's daughter Padmaja Naidu was a freedom fighter who later served as Governor of West Bengal.

questions.q: Sarojini Naidu was associated with which 1917 delegation that met the Secretary of State to demand voting rights for women?
options: A women's deputation to Edwin Montagu
questions.explanation: In 1917 Sarojini Naidu was part of a women's deputation that met Secretary of State Edwin Montagu to seek women's franchise.

questions.q: Which mentor figure of the early Congress encouraged the young Sarojini Naidu's entry into nationalist politics?
options: Dadabhai Naoroji and Gopal Krishna Gokhale
questions.explanation: Early leaders including Dadabhai Naoroji and Gopal Krishna Gokhale encouraged Sarojini Naidu's entry into nationalist politics.

questions.q: Sarojini Naidu was a child prodigy who reportedly passed her matriculation examination at the University of Madras at what young age?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu was a prodigy who passed the matriculation examination at the University of Madras around the age of twelve.

questions.q: Sarojini Naidu's posthumously published poetry collection, edited by her daughter, was titled:
questions.explanation: The Feather of the Dawn was Sarojini Naidu's posthumous poetry collection, edited by her daughter Padmaja.
